ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the market sector (e.g., Technology, Healthcare) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sector */,
  "sector_type" TEXT /* Type of sector (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  "parent_sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent sector (for sub-sectors) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each news source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the news source (e.g., Bloomberg, Reuters) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of source (e.g., online, print, broadcast) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries regarding the news source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the news source */,
  "source_reliability_score" REAL /* Reliability score of the news source (e.g., 0-1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_articles" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each news article */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the news source */,
  "headline" TEXT /* Headline of the news article */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the news article */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date of publication */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the news article */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_news_articles_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES news_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the analysis method (e.g., Natural Language Processing) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of how the method analyzes sentiment */,
  "algorithm_used" TEXT /* Algorithm used by the method */,
  "accuracy_score" REAL /* Accuracy score of the method (e.g., 0-1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sector_sentiment" (
  "sector_sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector sentiment entry */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sector to which this data belongs */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the sentiment analysis for the sector */,
  "sentiment_score" INTEGER /* Overall sentiment score for the sector on that date */,
  "sentiment_high" INTEGER /* Highest sentiment score recorded that day */,
  "sentiment_low" INTEGER /* Lowest sentiment score recorded that day */,
  "news_volume" INTEGER /* Number of news articles analyzed */,
  "news_buzz" INTEGER /* Measurement of news buzz based on volume and social media presence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sector_sentiment_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_data" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment entry */,
  "sector_sentiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sector sentiment to which this data belongs */,
  "article_id" INTEGER /* ID of the news article */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sentiment analysis method used */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the sentiment analysis */,
  "sentiment_score" INTEGER /* Overall sentiment score for the day */,
  "sentiment_high" INTEGER /* Highest sentiment score recorded that day */,
  "sentiment_low" INTEGER /* Lowest sentiment score recorded that day */,
  "news_volume" INTEGER /* Number of news articles analyzed */,
  "news_buzz" INTEGER /* Measurement of news buzz based on volume and social media presence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_data_sector_sentiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_sentiment_id") REFERENCES sector_sentiment ("sector_sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_data_article_id FOREIGN KEY ("article_id") REFERENCES news_articles ("article_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_data_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES sentiment_analysis_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, trader, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sentiment data */,
  "date_accessed" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action taken (e.g., view, download) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource that was accessed (e.g., sentiment data, reports) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was created */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report findings */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report parameter */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., date range, sector) */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_parameters_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sector_performance" (
  "sector_performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector performance entry */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sector */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the performance data */,
  "performance_score" REAL /* Performance score of the sector on that date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_performance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_indices" (
  "index_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market index */,
  "index_name" TEXT /* Name of the market index (e.g., S&P 500, Dow Jones) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the market index */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("index_id")
)
